title: copywriter path placement
description: A short quiz on the core ideas of the Copywriter path. Answer what you know. Each chapter you place out of is one you can skip ahead on, and you can revisit any chapter any time.

scoring.chapterSkipRule: Each chapter is tested by 2 questions worth 2 points each. A learner places out of a chapter (skips its Version A) by answering both of its questions correctly. Overall, 50 percent or more of maxScore recommends Version B as the default for chapters not individually skipped.

questions.prompt: You want a tool that turns a product brief into five headline options, run on demand, without you opening a chat each time. What makes that possible?

options.label: Calling an AI model through its API from code, so the tool runs the model itself
Nothing, copy can only come from a chat window
A bigger thesaurus
Saving your favorite chat as a template

questions.prompt: An AI model drafts marketing copy that includes a specific statistic. What should you assume?

options.label: The statistic might be made up, so you verify any factual claim before it ships
If the model wrote it confidently, it is true
Models never include numbers
Statistics from AI are always correct

questions.prompt: You want AI-drafted copy to sound like your brand. What is the most reliable approach?

options.label: Describe the voice in the prompt and show the model examples of on-brand copy
Just write "make it on brand" and trust the model knows your brand
Accept whatever voice it uses
Use more exclamation points

questions.prompt: The draft came back generic and bland. What is the best first fix?

options.label: Sharpen the prompt: add the audience, the angle, the constraints, and a strong example
Run the same prompt again unchanged
Decide the model cannot write
Make the prompt as short as possible

questions.prompt: Your brand has a detailed style guide. You want the model to write copy that follows it. What is the right move?

options.label: Give the model the relevant parts of the style guide as context for the task
Assume the model already memorized your private style guide
Keep the style guide secret from the model
Style guides cannot be used with AI

questions.prompt: Why does giving the model your own documents as context produce better results than not?

options.label: The model can only use what it is given or already trained on; your private docs are not in its training
It does not help, the model knows everything already
It only makes the model slower
Context is just for images

questions.prompt: AI-written copy reads beautifully and confidently states your product won an award you have never heard of. What do you do?

options.label: Treat it as possibly invented and verify the claim before the copy goes anywhere
Leave it in, it sounds impressive and confident
Assume the model researched it for you
Add more awards to match

questions.prompt: Which part of AI-generated copy most deserves a careful fact-check?

options.label: Any specific claim: names, numbers, dates, quotes, and features
Only the spelling
Nothing, confident copy is reliable copy
Only the headline
